key_departures.impact: Super Bowl LX MVP and the team's featured back. Walker posted 730+ scrimmage yards and 20 touchdowns in the regular season and over 300 rushing yards with 4 TDs in the playoffs. Departs to become the highest-paid RB in league history per reports. Zach Charbonnet is recovering from a torn ACL, leaving only depth options (George Holani, Kenny McIntosh, Cam Akers) as healthy backfield options.

Versatile safety/slot CB hybrid who logged 4 INTs and 41 tackles in 2025 and 7 interceptions over his last two seasons. Was Seattle's ball-hawking defensive back and a key piece of the top-ranked scoring defense. Departure leaves a genuine hole at the starting safety spot opposite Julian Love.

2022 second-round pick who racked up 146 pressures across three seasons, ranking 31st at the position over that span per PFF. Only 2 sacks in 2025 but ranked No. 22 among all free agents by PFF due to elite pass-rush win rate. Was a rotational edge rusher (not a full-time starter) but played in 38% of playoff snaps. Departure escalates EDGE depth concern but DeMarcus Lawrence, Derick Hall, and Uchenna Nwosu remain.

Former Pro Bowl corner who led the NFL with 6 INTs as a rookie in 2022 and totaled 6 INTs over the last three seasons. Lost his starting spot to Josh Jobe in 2025 but remained a key piece of the secondary. Seattle let him walk, retaining Jobe instead. The 1-year Eagles deal suggests even Philly views him as a prove-it signing. CB2 spot (outside Witherspoon and Jobe) is now thin.
Special teams standout. Depth loss only. LB need picture unchanged.
Primarily a special teams contributor. Minimal offensive role. Depth/ST loss only.

Seahawks declined to tender Bobo as an RFA, making him a UFA. Caught only 2 passes in the regular season but contributed on special teams including an NFC Championship TD catch. Expect Seattle to try to re-sign on a lower deal. Minimal offensive impact.

signings.analysis: Shaheed re-signs on a 3yr/$51M deal with $34.7M guaranteed per Adam Schefter. Cap hits of $9.666M (2026), $19.166M (2027), $22.166M (2028). WR was not a critical need entering FA — JSN, Kupp, Shaheed, and Tory Horton give Darnold a deep room. Re-signing keeps the position fully addressed. The $17M AAV is starter-tier for WR. Note: Shaheed was primarily a return specialist and big-play threat after the midseason trade from New Orleans; the expectation is a bigger offensive role in 2026 under new OC Brian Fleury.

Jobe re-signs on a 3yr/$24M deal. Contract structure: $7M signing bonus, $2.25M guaranteed salary in 2026, with a Feb. 10, 2027 trigger date for $5M of the 2027 salary to vest fully guaranteed. Cap hits: $4.533M (2026), $9.333M (2027), $10.083M (2028). Jobe was the NFL's 4th-lowest completion-percentage-allowed CB (47.7%, min. 50 targets) and 4th-fewest yards per target (5.4) among outside CBs in 2025. He solidifies the CB2 role behind Devon Witherspoon. However, the loss of Riq Woolen leaves the CB3 spot thin (Nehemiah Pritchett is the next man up). CB is partially addressed — starter locked in, but depth is a live concern going into the draft.

Thomas agreed to a 2yr/$8M base extension (up to $9M with incentives) per Ian Rapoport. Was a restricted free agent who avoided the tender process with a deal. Had 96 tackles and 3.5 sacks as a first-year starter in 2025, adding 18 tackles during the Super Bowl run. Paired with Ernest Jones IV, the Seahawks retain both starting LBs. LB was not a need going into FA and remains fully addressed. $4M AAV is below-market for a starter but reflects his RFA status — this is a team-friendly deal.

Lock returns to Seattle on a 2yr/$5M deal signed in April 2025 (already under contract entering 2026 FA period). Was Seattle's backup QB in 2022-23 before leaving. Now provides insurance behind Sam Darnold. Contract is minimum-adjacent at $2.5M AAV. QB is not a need with Darnold signed as the clear starter. Lock is a known commodity as a backup — this does not change any positional need picture.

Bell signed a 1yr/$1.37M deal (signed March 24, 2025 — was on the roster for the Super Bowl run). Primarily a special teams standout who blocked a punt against the Saints in 2025. Logged 14 appearances (2 starts), 0.5 sacks, 15 tackles. Not a starter-caliber safety. With Coby Bryant gone and Julian Love the clear starter, Bell provides depth/ST value but does NOT address the vacancy left by Bryant. Safety remains a meaningful need despite Bell's presence.

Saubert had his contract extended for an additional year in December 2025 per Seahawks.com. A journeyman TE (Falcons, Bears, Jaguars, Broncos, Cowboys, Texans, 49ers) who provides depth behind Noah Fant. TE is not a pressing need with Fant locked in as the starter. Saubert is a blocking TE and depth piece only.

signings.previous_team: Seattle Seahawks (re-sign — was waiver claim from Baltimore)
signings.analysis: Ross re-signed after appearing in 10 games on special teams in 2025 (67% of ST snaps, 4 tackles). Was claimed off waivers from Baltimore in-season. A special teams ace who has a prior relationship with Mike Macdonald from both Michigan and the Ravens. Purely a ST/depth signing. Does not affect LB need picture.

Signed March 21, 2025 — under contract entering 2026 FA period. Seven-year veteran with 53 career games, 32 catches for 374 yards. Former WSU product who can also handle punt returns. Pure depth/ST body in a WR room already featuring JSN, Kupp, Shaheed, and Horton. Minimal positional impact.

signings.notes: Signed as WR depth and a candidate to handle kick and punt returns after the departures of Rashid Shaheed (re-signed) and Dareke Young. Spent time with Commanders, Steelers, Texans, Ravens. Veteran minimum-range deal. Also serves as a return option.

signings.analysis: Tendered as an Exclusive Rights Free Agent — can only sign with Seattle at the tender rate. Healthy depth back alongside McIntosh, Akers, etc. with Zach Charbonnet on ACL recovery. Does NOT address the starter void left by Kenneth Walker III. RB remains a high-priority need for the draft.
Tendered as an ERFA — can only sign with Seattle. Young developmental safety who provides depth. Does not fill Coby Bryant's starting role. Safety remains a genuine need.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Kenneth Walker III (Super Bowl MVP) departed to Kansas City on a 3yr/$45M deal — the highest-paid RB in league history at signing. Zach Charbonnet is recovering from a torn ACL. The only healthy backs on roster are depth bodies: George Holani (ERFA tender), Kenny McIntosh, Cam Akers, Velus Jones Jr. No meaningful FA signing was made at the position. RB rockets to a Tier 1 draft need. This is the biggest hole created by free agency and a near-certain Day 1 or Day 2 draft target.

Coby Bryant signed a 3yr/$40M deal with the Bears ($25.75M guaranteed). He was Seattle's starting ball-hawking safety who produced 7 INTs over the last two seasons. Julian Love is the clear starter, but the No. 2 safety role is now unmanned. D'Anthony Bell (minimum/ST player) and ERFA Ty Okada are not starter-quality replacements. S escalates to a Tier 2 need — rounds 2-4 or a later free agent addition.

Partially addressed by the Josh Jobe re-signing (3yr/$24M). Jobe solidifies CB2 behind Devon Witherspoon. However, Riq Woolen's departure to Philadelphia leaves the CB3 spot thin — Nehemiah Pritchett (2024 5th-round pick) is the only realistic option and hasn't proven capable of a starting role. CB remains Tier 2: starter depth is needed, ideally in rounds 2-4 of the draft. Need is unchanged despite Jobe's retention — the LOSS of Woolen exactly offsets the RETENTION of Jobe.

Boye Mafe's departure (3yr/$60M to Bengals) escalates edge depth slightly. However, DeMarcus Lawrence (signed last offseason), Derick Hall, and Uchenna Nwosu remain. The Seahawks have a functional starting edge rotation even without Mafe, who was rotational (38% of playoff snaps). This drops from a mild Tier 2 to a Tier 3 — they should add a developmental EDGE in the mid-rounds but it is not an urgent Day 1/2 need.

Rashid Shaheed retained on a 3yr/$51M deal with $34.7M guaranteed. WR room: JSN (extension pending), Cooper Kupp ($13.5M cap hit), Shaheed, Tory Horton, plus depth. This is a fully stacked room for a run-first team. WR drops to Tier 4 — luxury pick only. Day 3 developmental talent at most.

Both starting LBs retained: Drake Thomas (2yr/$8M extension) and Ernest Jones IV (already under contract). Thomas had 96 tackles and 3.5 sacks as a first-year starter in 2025. LB is fully addressed. Tier 4 — no meaningful draft investment needed here.
Sam Darnold is the unquestioned starter under a multi-year deal. Drew Lock returns as the backup on a 2yr/$5M deal. QB room is stable. No change needed.

key_additions.headline: Playoff hero and Pro Bowl returner re-signs, keeping Seattle's receiver room elite and returning specialist intact
UDFA-to-starter locked up at below-market value; posted 4th-lowest completion % allowed by any CB in 2025
Starting LB retained on team-friendly extension; 96 tackles and 3.5 sacks in first year as a starter
Familiar backup returns to back up Darnold at minimum cost after two seasons in Seattle 2022-23
Special teams standout and safety depth re-signed after Super Bowl run; punt block against Saints was a season highlight
ERFA tender keeps healthy depth back on roster as team searches for a Walker replacement

key_losses.contract_summary: 3yr/$45M with Chiefs (est. highest-paid RB at signing)
key_losses.headline: Super Bowl LX MVP departs as the Seahawks' biggest loss; 730 scrimmage yards and 20 TDs in the regular season, 300+ rushing yards in the playoffs
Ball-hawking safety/CB hybrid with 7 INTs over last two seasons leaves a starter-sized hole opposite Julian Love
2022 2nd-round pick cashes in with Bengals; 146 pressures over three seasons, top-25 pass-rush win rate in the NFL
Former Pro Bowl CB and 2022 INT leader (6) departs on a prove-it deal; Josh Jobe's retention makes Woolen expendable

notes: All major signings and departures confirmed through NFL.com, ESPN, OverTheCap, Spotrac, and Seahawks.com as of March 11, 2026. Drew Lock, D'Anthony Bell, River Cracraft, Steven Sims, and Josh Ross were signed in 2025 offseason and remain under contract; included as relevant to 2026 roster context. Contract guarantees for Jobe sourced from Seattle Times salary cap breakdown and Field Gulls/OverTheCap detail. Guaranteed money for Drake Thomas extension not fully published — listed as TBD. Steven Sims, River Cracraft, and Josh Ross contract values are minimum-range deals with terms not yet publicly reported in detail. Cap space post-FA reflects OverTheCap figure of $42,966,262 as of March 11, 2026, which must still accommodate rookie class and in-season roster needs. Pending extensions for Jaxon Smith-Njigba and Devon Witherspoon will significantly reduce remaining cap space.
